libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, API key, or external AI service is needed. Exercise the auto-configuration with ApplicationContextRunner and a local stub ChatModel bean. For 2.0.0, ChatClientBuilderProperties.ToolCalling exposes enabled and advisorOrder only; do not use the removed stream-tool-call-responses property or isStreamToolCallResponses() accessor.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides ChatClient, ChatModel, advisors, prompt/message types, and observation handler classes used by ChatClientAutoConfiguration.
Provides ToolCallingAutoConfiguration and tool-calling auto-configuration types referenced by the chat client auto-configuration.
Provides Spring Boot auto-configuration annotations and AutoConfigurations support needed to load and exercise the module.
Provides ApplicationContextRunner, the normal lightweight way to test this auto-configuration without a full application.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8744 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-client:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing RC2 test pins Spring AI and Spring Boot support dependencies to RC versions; mixing those with the 2.0.0 artifact caused the observed javac failure.
Adding micrometer-tracing is optional and changes which observation nested configuration activates; do not add it unless the generated tests intentionally cover the tracer-present branch.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a Spring Boot auto-configuration module whose main collaborators are optional Maven dependencies, so the normal scaffold cannot reach meaningful ChatClient auto-configuration coverage without adding them explicitly.
